section_e1.name: Parameter Identification (MCQ)
section_e1.instructions: Select the best Serum parameter(s) to adjust for the given sound design goal.

questions.question: To make a bass sound 'fatter' with more width in Serum, which parameters should you primarily adjust?

options.A: Oscillator A unison voices and detune amount

questions.rationale: Unison stacks multiple detuned copies of the oscillator, creating a fatter, wider sound. More voices and detune = thicker bass.

questions.question: To create a 'plucky' sound with a quick transient in Serum, which envelope settings are most critical?

options.A: Long attack, long decay, high sustain
options.B: Zero attack, short decay, low sustain
options.C: Medium attack, no decay, maximum sustain
options.D: Long attack, no decay, zero sustain

questions.rationale: Plucky sounds need instant onset (zero attack), quick fade (short decay), and minimal held volume (low sustain). This creates the sharp transient characteristic of plucks.

questions.question: In Serum, what does the 'WT Pos' (wavetable position) parameter control?

options.A: The volume level of the wavetable oscillator
options.B: Which frame of the wavetable is currently playing
options.C: The stereo position of the wavetable in the mix
options.D: The pitch offset of the wavetable in semitones

questions.rationale: WT Pos scrolls through different frames/shapes in the wavetable. Modulating it creates evolving timbres as different waveforms are scanned.

questions.question: To add movement and wobble to a dubstep bass in Serum, you would typically modulate the filter cutoff with:

options.B: An LFO synced to the tempo
options.C: The velocity of incoming MIDI notes

questions.rationale: Dubstep wobble comes from rhythmic filter movement. A tempo-synced LFO on filter cutoff creates the classic wobble effect at musically relevant rates.

questions.question: To make a lead sound 'scream' or self-oscillate in Serum, which parameter should be cranked up?
questions.rationale: High resonance creates a peak at the cutoff frequency. At extreme values, the filter self-oscillates, producing a screaming or whistling tone.

questions.question: In Serum, the 'Warp' modes (Sync, Bend, Mirror, etc.) affect:

options.B: How the wavetable waveform is distorted/transformed

questions.rationale: Warp modes transform the oscillator waveform in various ways - Sync adds harmonics, Bend reshapes the wave, Mirror creates symmetric distortion, etc.

questions.question: To create a sub bass that sits well in a mix without mud, which Serum oscillator would you primarily use?
questions.rationale: The Sub oscillator generates clean, simple waveforms (sine, triangle) perfect for sub bass. Complex wavetables add harmonics that can muddy the low end.

questions.question: To create a 'supersaw' lead sound in Serum, which combination of settings is most effective?

options.A: Sawtooth wavetable + high unison voices + moderate detune
options.B: Sine wavetable + no unison + heavy distortion
options.D: Square wavetable + single voice + chorus effect

questions.rationale: Classic supersaw uses sawtooth waveforms with multiple unison voices slightly detuned. This creates the thick, wide, iconic trance/EDM lead sound.

questions.question: The 'Semi' and 'Fine' parameters in Serum's oscillator section control:

options.B: Oscillator pitch in semitones and cents

questions.rationale: Semi adjusts pitch by semitones (half steps), Fine adjusts by cents (1/100th of a semitone). Used for detuning oscillators against each other or transposing.

questions.question: In Serum, to route Envelope 2 to modulate the filter cutoff, you would:

options.A: Click the Env2 source and drag to the filter cutoff knob
options.C: Enable the 'Auto-Mod' button on the filter

questions.rationale: Serum's modulation matrix uses drag-and-drop. Click the modulation source (Env2) and drag to any destination parameter to create a modulation routing.

section_e2.name: Parameter Adjustment (Open-Ended)
section_e2.instructions: Describe the specific Serum parameters you would adjust and explain why.

questions.question: A producer wants to turn a static pad sound into an evolving, atmospheric texture in Serum. What parameters would you suggest modulating, and how?

scoring_criteria.3: Mentions WT position modulation with slow LFO, filter movement, possibly reverb/delay tails
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions at least two relevant modulation targets (WT pos, filter, etc.) with LFO
scoring_criteria.1: Mentions modulation but vague on specifics

questions.question: How would you create a punchy 808-style kick bass in Serum? Describe the oscillator, envelope, and any other relevant settings.

scoring_criteria.3: Describes sine/sub osc, pitch envelope dropping quickly, short amp decay, possibly noise for click
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions sine wave and envelope shaping for punch
scoring_criteria.1: General understanding of needing low frequencies but lacks specific technique

questions.question: Explain how you would set up a tempo-synced filter wobble for a dubstep bass in Serum.

scoring_criteria.3: Details LFO synced to BPM (1/4, 1/8 etc), routed to filter cutoff with appropriate depth
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions LFO to filter cutoff with tempo sync
scoring_criteria.1: Mentions LFO or filter but missing sync concept

questions.question: A sound is too harsh and digital. What Serum parameters would you adjust to warm it up and make it more analog-sounding?

scoring_criteria.3: Suggests lowpass filter to cut highs, subtle saturation/drive, possibly adding sub, reducing harsh wavetable frames
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions filtering highs and/or adding warmth through saturation
scoring_criteria.1: General concept of reducing harshness but no specific parameters

questions.question: How would you create stereo width on a mono lead sound in Serum without using external plugins?

scoring_criteria.3: Mentions unison with detune spread to stereo, possibly Hyper/Dimension effect, or stereo filter modes
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions unison spread or built-in stereo effects
scoring_criteria.1: Understands width needed but vague on Serum-specific solutions

questions.question: Describe how to create a 'growl' or 'talking bass' effect in Serum using FM modulation.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains FM routing (Osc B to A), using vocal/formant wavetables, filter/WT movement for vowel sounds
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions FM between oscillators for growl texture
scoring_criteria.1: Knows FM creates complex tones but lacks growl-specific technique

questions.question: What parameters would you adjust to make a sound's attack more aggressive and 'snappy' in Serum?

scoring_criteria.3: Zero attack on amp envelope, possibly adding noise/click layer, fast filter envelope for brightness on hit
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions fast/zero attack and possibly transient shaping
scoring_criteria.1: Understands attack affects snappiness but lacks detail

questions.question: How would you set up velocity sensitivity in Serum so harder key presses result in brighter sounds?

scoring_criteria.3: Route Velocity source to filter cutoff (positive amount) in mod matrix, possibly also to volume or WT pos
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions velocity modulating filter or brightness
scoring_criteria.1: Knows velocity affects dynamics but not brightness routing

questions.question: Explain how to use Serum's noise oscillator to add 'air' or breathiness to a pad sound.

scoring_criteria.3: Enable noise osc at low level, high-pass filter it to keep only airy highs, blend subtly with main oscillators
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions noise oscillator filtered for air/texture
scoring_criteria.1: Knows noise adds texture but lacks filtering concept

questions.question: How would you recreate a classic Reese bass sound in Serum?

scoring_criteria.3: Two saw oscillators slightly detuned (few cents), creating phasing movement, possibly unison, low-passed
scoring_criteria.2: Mentions detuned saws for phasing effect
scoring_criteria.1: Knows it involves bass oscillators but misses detuning technique

section_e3.name: Preset Analysis (Scenario)
section_e3.instructions: Analyze the given Serum preset parameters and explain the sound design choices.

questions.scenario:
You're given a bass preset with these key parameters:
- Oscillator A: Volume 0.78, WT Position 0.35, Warp Mode: FM (from B)
- Oscillator B: Volume 0.0, Semi: -12 (one octave down)
- Filter: Low-pass, Cutoff 0.4, Resonance 0.3
- Unison: 4 voices, Detune 0.25

What type of bass sound does this create, and why are these settings chosen?
questions.question: Analyze this preset configuration and explain what type of bass sound it produces.

scoring_criteria.3: Identifies FM bass where Osc B modulates A, B being silent but adding harmonics, filtered for control, unison for fatness
scoring_criteria.2: Recognizes FM interaction between oscillators and general bass character
scoring_criteria.1: Identifies as bass sound but misses FM relationship

questions.scenario:
A lead preset has these settings:
- Oscillator A: Saw wavetable, WT Pos 1.0, Unison 7 voices, Detune 0.15
- Filter: Cutoff 0.85, Resonance 0.6
- Envelope 1 (Amp): Attack 0ms, Decay 200ms, Sustain 0.7
- LFO1 to Filter Cutoff: Rate 1/4, Amount 0.3

What sound does this create?
questions.question: What type of lead sound do these settings produce? Explain the role of each major parameter group.

scoring_criteria.3: Identifies supersaw lead, explains unison for thickness, high cutoff for brightness, LFO adds rhythmic filter movement
scoring_criteria.2: Recognizes supersaw character and filter modulation
scoring_criteria.1: Identifies as lead but misses supersaw or modulation details

questions.scenario:
A pad preset configuration:
- Oscillator A: WT Position modulated by LFO1 (slow, 0.1 Hz)
- Oscillator B: -7 semitones, WT Position modulated by LFO2 (different rate)
- Filter: Low-pass, Cutoff 0.5, modulated by Env2 (slow attack)
- FX: Reverb 60%, Delay 30%
- Unison: 3 voices each osc, Detune 0.1

Analyze this pad sound.
questions.question: What makes this an effective pad sound? Explain how the modulation creates the evolving texture.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains slow WT modulation creates evolution, -7 semi adds fifth harmony, different LFO rates prevent static sound, FX add space
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies evolving pad with modulation and effects
scoring_criteria.1: Recognizes as pad but doesn't explain modulation purpose

questions.scenario:
An 808-style bass preset:
- Sub Oscillator: Sine, Level 0.9
- Oscillator A: Sine at WT Pos 0, Level 0.4
- Pitch Envelope: -24 semitones range, Decay 150ms
- Amp Envelope: Attack 0ms, Decay 1.5s, Sustain 0.0
- No filter active

Why these specific settings for an 808?
questions.question: Explain the sound design reasoning behind this 808 kick/bass configuration.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains pitch envelope creates the 'boom' pitch drop, sine waves keep it clean/pure, long decay for sustained sub, no filter to preserve lows
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies pitch envelope for 808 character and sine for clean sub
scoring_criteria.1: Recognizes 808 sound but misses pitch envelope importance

questions.scenario:
A dubstep 'growl' bass preset:
- Oscillator A: Vocal formant wavetable, WT Pos modulated by LFO (1/8 rate)
- Oscillator B: FM Carrier, modulating Osc A
- Filter: Band-pass, Cutoff modulated by same LFO
- Distortion: Tube mode, Drive 40%

How do these elements create the growl character?
questions.question: Break down how this preset achieves the 'growl' or 'talking bass' effect.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains formant wavetable provides vowel shapes, FM adds complexity, bandpass emphasizes formants, LFO creates rhythmic 'talking', distortion adds grit
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies formant/FM interaction creating growl with modulation
scoring_criteria.1: Recognizes growl sound but doesn't explain mechanism

questions.scenario:
A pluck/key preset:
- Oscillator A: Basic Shapes wavetable (position 0.3)
- Filter: Low-pass 24dB, Cutoff 0.2, Env2 amount +0.7
- Envelope 2: Attack 0ms, Decay 180ms, Sustain 0.0
- Amp Envelope: Attack 0ms, Decay 300ms, Sustain 0.0
- FX: Small room reverb 20%

What creates the 'plucky' character here?
questions.question: Explain what makes this preset sound plucky and how the envelopes work together.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains Env2 opens filter quickly then closes (brightness on attack), amp envelope slightly longer for body, zero sustain for pluck character
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies filter envelope creating brightness decay for pluck
scoring_criteria.1: Knows envelopes affect pluckiness but doesn't explain interaction

questions.scenario:
A 'hyper' lead preset:
- Oscillator A: Basic Saw, 16 unison voices, Detune 0.4
- Hyper/Dimension effect enabled at 50%
- Filter: High-pass at 200Hz, then Low-pass at 8kHz
- No modulation on wavetable position

What does this create and why so many unison voices?
questions.question: Analyze this 'hyper' lead configuration. What's the purpose of the extreme unison and dual filtering?

scoring_criteria.3: Explains 16 unison creates massive width/thickness, HP removes mud, LP controls harshness, Hyper adds dimension, static WT keeps it stable
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies massive unison for width with filtering for control
scoring_criteria.1: Knows it's a wide lead but doesn't explain filtering purpose

questions.scenario:
A sub bass preset:
- Sub Oscillator: Sine, Level 1.0
- Oscillator A: Disabled
- Oscillator B: Disabled
- Filter: Disabled (bypass)
- No unison, no effects

Why would a producer use such a minimal preset?
questions.question: Explain the design philosophy behind this extremely minimal sub bass configuration.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains pure sine = no harmonics to muddy mix, used as dedicated sub layer under other elements, no processing to maintain phase/clarity
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies pure sub purpose for clean low end
scoring_criteria.1: Recognizes it's simple but doesn't explain layering strategy

questions.scenario:
A 'screaming' lead preset:
- Oscillator A: Saw + Resonance wavetable
- Filter: Low-pass, Cutoff 0.6, Resonance 0.95 (near max)
- LFO1 to Cutoff: Slow rate (2 bars), Amount 0.3
- Distortion: Soft clip, Drive 60%

What creates the 'scream' in this sound?
questions.question: How does this preset achieve the 'screaming' lead character?

scoring_criteria.3: Explains near-max resonance creates sharp peak/near self-oscillation at cutoff, slow LFO sweeps this 'scream' point, distortion adds harmonics and aggression
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies high resonance as source of scream with modulation
scoring_criteria.1: Knows it sounds aggressive but misses resonance role

questions.scenario:
A trap hi-hat style preset:
- Noise Oscillator: White noise, Level 0.8
- Oscillator A/B: Disabled
- Filter: High-pass at 6kHz, Resonance 0.2
- Amp Envelope: Attack 0ms, Decay 80ms, Sustain 0.0
- No effects

How does this create a hi-hat sound from Serum?
questions.question: Explain the sound design approach for creating hi-hats in a synth like Serum.

scoring_criteria.3: Explains white noise = all frequencies, HP filter isolates high frequencies like metal, short decay creates closed hi-hat character
scoring_criteria.2: Identifies noise + filtering + short envelope for hi-hat
scoring_criteria.1: Knows it uses noise but doesn't explain filtering purpose
